diagnosis:
The volatility_squeeze strategy implements a TTM Squeeze variant: it detects when Bollinger Bands (20-period, 2.0 std) are nested inside a Keltner Channel (20-period EMA, ATR x keltner_atr_mult), waits for the squeeze to hold for at least squeeze_min_bars, then enters when the squeeze releases. Direction is determined by a linear regression slope over the last momentum_lookback bars. Entry also requires a volume_ratio surge above volume_surge_threshold and an optional ADX floor (currently adx_min=0.0, effectively disabled). Regime gate: only enters in "breakout" or "lateral" regimes.

The strategy EMITS SHORTS (slope < 0 branch) and must be converted to long-or-flat. The short branch at lines 108-118 must be dropped; a negative slope at squeeze release becomes a cash/no-trade signal.

The evolved PARAMS (gen 472, 5375 evals) show a tight Keltner multiplier (1.1686) that catches narrow squeezes, a relatively high volume bar (1.9x), a wide stop (3.79 ATR) and modest TP (4.60 ATR), and a 36-hour time stop. The claim of Sharpe=3.31 WF=3.94 over 49 trades is suspicious—49 trades over any multi-year horizon is sparse, suggesting the edge is selective but plausible. The core edge is genuine: post-squeeze vol expansions in crypto tend to trend, and requiring volume confirmation reduces false releases. Weakness: (1) the slope direction is computed on pre-release bars, which can disagree with the actual expansion direction in choppy releases; (2) the short arm captures downside moves that in spot long-only would simply be missed—converting to long-only reduces signal frequency by roughly half; (3) the volume_surge_threshold=1.9 is very high and will miss many legitimate releases; (4) the time_stop=36h is very short for a breakout that may run for days.
long_only_hypothesis: After a verified volatility compression (BB inside Keltner for at least N bars) releases to the upside, crypto spot assets exhibit sustained directional momentum that lasts longer than the time_stop currently allows. The long-only hypothesis is: enter long when the squeeze releases AND the linear regression slope AND close-vs-recent-high confirm upward momentum AND volume surges, hold with a trailing stop or wider time stop, exit to cash otherwise. The informational asymmetry is that crypto spot has a positive drift bias (2019-2026 BTC/ETH compound ~+60% CAGR), so capturing the upside squeeze releases and sitting in cash on the downside releases should outperform the flat short arm net-net, especially after avoiding short-side funding and slippage costs.
market_techniques: TTM Squeeze (Bollinger inside Keltner) for compression detection
Linear regression slope for directional momentum at release
Volume surge confirmation to filter false releases
Regime gating to restrict entries to breakout and lateral markets
bb_bw_percentile rolling rank for squeeze depth scoring
Walk-forward validation with embargo gaps for parameter robustness
BMSB (Bull Market Support Band) as a macro trend regime filter
Trailing stop to let winning breakouts run beyond the current tight 36h time stop
ADX threshold as post-release trend strength gate
useful_features: volatility: bb_bw_percentile — core squeeze depth; low percentile = deep squeeze
volatility: bb_bandwidth — raw bandwidth to detect squeeze exit by expansion delta
volatility: atr_14 — stop and take-profit sizing, Keltner channel width
volatility: gk_vol — Garman-Klass for a cleaner realized vol estimate during compression
volatility: vol_ratio (short/long realized vol) — secondary compression indicator
trend: adx_14 — post-release trend strength gate to avoid whipsaw releases
trend: bmsb_bullish — macro bull-market filter; only enter longs when above BMSB
momentum: roc_12 — 12-bar rate of change as supplementary directional signal
momentum: rsi_14 — avoid entries when overbought (rsi_14 > 70) at squeeze release
momentum: price_acceleration — second derivative of close, confirms momentum building
volume: volume_ratio — primary volume surge gate (already used)
volume: volume_zscore — z-score based volume spike detector (more robust than raw ratio)
volume: obv_divergence — OBV divergence from its SMA signals accumulation during squeeze
price/return: dist_from_high_20 — proximity to recent 20-bar high; breakout above confirms direction
price/return: close_location — high close_location (near high) at release bar confirms bullish push
market regime: regime label (breakout/lateral) — already used; keep as gate
sentiment/funding (informative only): funding rate direction as a filter — do NOT enter long when funding is extremely negative (capitulation signal); treat as warning flag, not PnL source

genome_params.name: keltner_atr_mult
genome_params.note: Controls how tight the Keltner must be to detect a squeeze. Current evolved value 1.1686 is on the tight end; allow evolution to explore looser definitions.

genome_params.name: squeeze_min_bars
genome_params.note: Minimum bars of compression before qualifying as a squeeze. Current=3; allow deeper search for sustained squeezes.

genome_params.name: momentum_lookback
genome_params.note: Linear regression window for slope direction. Current=13; let evolution find longer/shorter memory.

genome_params.name: volume_surge_threshold
genome_params.note: Volume ratio at release must exceed this. Current=1.9; range allows evolution to relax for rare-but-strong setups or tighten for higher precision.

genome_params.name: stop_loss_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR multiplier for stop loss. Current=3.79; breakout strategies need wide stops.

genome_params.name: take_profit_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR multiplier for take profit. Current=4.60; allow evolution to let winners run further in long-only context.

genome_params.name: time_stop_hours
genome_params.note: Maximum hold time. Current=36h is too short for multi-day breakouts; extend range to allow 5-day holds.

genome_params.name: adx_min
genome_params.note: ADX floor for trend confirmation post-release. Currently 0 (disabled); allow evolution to enable it as a quality filter.

genome_params.name: squeeze_depth_pct_max
genome_params.note: NEW: maximum bb_bw_percentile allowed during squeeze bars. Currently strategy uses hard < 20 threshold; make evolvable.

genome_params.name: rsi_overbought_filter
genome_params.note: NEW: skip long entry if rsi_14 > this at squeeze release (avoids buying exhausted moves). Not currently in strategy.

do_not_optimize: bb_period (fixed at 20 — TTM Squeeze standard; changing it redefines the indicator)
bb_std (fixed at 2.0 — TTM Squeeze standard; deviating creates a non-standard indicator with arbitrary squeeze definition)
keltner_period (fixed at 20 — must match bb_period for the squeeze comparison to be meaningful)
require_volume_surge (keep True — the vol confirmation is the main noise filter; toggling it adds a free bool that invites overfitting)
allowed_regimes (keep ['breakout', 'lateral'] — changes regime list without a principled basis invite curve fitting to regime labels)

risk_management.stop: ATR-based hard stop: close - stop_loss_atr_mult * atr_14. Range 1.5-5.0x ATR. The backtester enforces intrabar stop via low < stop_loss price check. This is the primary capital protection.
risk_management.take_profit: ATR-based take profit: close + take_profit_atr_mult * atr_14. Range 2.5-9.0x ATR in long-only mode to let breakouts run. TP must always exceed stop (constraint enforced in genome mutation).
risk_management.trailing: Optional ATR-based trailing stop: once unrealized gain exceeds 2.0x ATR, move stop to breakeven; once gain exceeds 3.0x ATR, trail at 1.5x ATR from rolling high. This replaces the rigid 36h time stop as the primary exit for winning trades.
risk_management.vol_target: Volatility-targeted position sizing via backtester risk_config.sizing_method = 'volatility_scaled'. Target annualized vol of 15-20% per position. At typical crypto ATR/price ~2-4% per bar, this implies position sizes of 0.15-0.30 of equity per trade.
risk_management.max_size: Maximum 25% of equity per single trade (config.max_position_pct = 0.25). Prevents any single squeeze release from dominating the portfolio.
risk_management.cooldown: Minimum 12 hours between new entries (risk_manager min_time_between_trades). This prevents the strategy from re-entering immediately after a time-stop exit, which could cause rapid drawdown in a choppy market.
risk_management.risk_off: Regime gate: skip entries if regime is not in ['breakout', 'lateral']. Additional macro gate: skip long entries if close < bmsb_sma (below Bull Market Support Band). Optional: skip if gk_vol z-score (20-bar) > 2.5 (extreme realized vol suggests market dislocation, not a tradeable squeeze release).

metrics: CAGR (annualized compound return on equity) — primary return metric
Sharpe ratio (annualized on hourly equity returns) — risk-adjusted return
Sortino ratio (downside deviation denominator) — relevant because distribution is right-skewed in breakout strategies
Maximum drawdown % (peak-to-trough on equity curve) — risk constraint; target < 20%
Calmar ratio (CAGR / maxDD) — combines return and tail risk in one scalar
Hit rate % (% trades with pnl_net > 0) — squeeze releases have directional bias; expect 45-60%
Profit factor (gross profit / gross loss) — should exceed 1.5 for viable breakout strategy
Average trade PnL net of all costs (commission + slippage) — must be clearly positive vs cost drag
Turnover (trades per month) — very low turnover (1-4/month) expected; verify costs are not killing the edge
Market exposure % (fraction of time in position) — target 15-35% for a selective breakout approach
Cost sensitivity: recompute Sharpe at 2x commission and 2x slippage to check robustness
Bootstrap/MC confidence interval on Sharpe (1000 iterations, trade-level resampling)
Per-calendar-year Sharpe and return — must show positive years across bull (2020-21, 2023-24) AND sideways (2022) markets
OOS degradation % (walk-forward test Sharpe vs train Sharpe) — target < 40% degradation
Deflated Sharpe Ratio (DSR) pass/fail — must clear dsr_min threshold after deflating by number of genome evaluations
sweep_changes: Remove the short branch (lines 108-118 in volatility_squeeze.py): replace 'elif slope < 0: signal = Signal(side="short", ...)' with 'return None' to convert to long-or-flat. Only emit Signal(side="long") when slope > 0.
Add rsi_overbought_filter parameter: before emitting long signal, check if current.get('rsi_14', 50) > p['rsi_overbought_filter']; if so, return None.
Add squeeze_depth_pct_max parameter: instead of hardcoding < 20 for bb_bw_percentile, use p['squeeze_depth_pct_max'] to make the squeeze threshold evolvable.
Add BMSB macro regime gate: add a check 'if p.get("require_bmsb_bullish", True) and not current.get("bmsb_bullish", True): return None' to restrict long entries to bull-market conditions.
Extend time_stop_hours range to [24, 120] in auto_evolve.py STRATEGY_REGISTRY param_space to allow multi-day breakout holds.
Add volume_zscore as secondary volume confirmation: 'if p.get("require_vol_zscore", False) and current.get("volume_zscore", 0) < p.get("vol_zscore_min", 1.5): return None'
Add obv_divergence filter: skip entry if obv_divergence < 0 (OBV below its 20-SMA) to confirm accumulation during the squeeze.

Update param_space in STRATEGY_REGISTRY to add: rsi_overbought_filter (float, 65.0, 85.0), squeeze_depth_pct_max (float, 10.0, 30.0), time_stop_hours (int, 24, 120). Also extend take_profit_atr_mult high to 9.0.

Add trailing stop logic: after position entry, if unrealized_pnl > 2.0 * atr_14 * size_per_unit, raise stop to entry. If unrealized_pnl > 3.0 * atr_14 * size_per_unit, trail at max(current_stop, high_since_entry - 1.5 * atr_14). This requires passing high_since_entry through the Position object.
Add dist_from_high_20 confirmation: require current.get('dist_from_high_20', -0.1) > -0.03 (price within 3% of 20-bar high) as upward breakout confirmation before entering.
methodological_risks: Sparse trade count risk: 49 trades over the evolved period yields high Sharpe uncertainty. The standard error of Sharpe ~ 1/sqrt(N) implies SE ~ 0.14 for 49 trades; a Sharpe of 3.31 ± 0.28 at 95% CI. Bootstrap CI must confirm significance.

Short-removal signal frequency halving: removing the short arm roughly halves the number of entries. The 49 original trades may drop to ~25 long-only trades, further widening Sharpe uncertainty and risking too few trades for walk-forward folds to be meaningful.

Regime label leakage risk: the strategy gates on regime labels. If regime labels are computed on future data or have lookahead within their rolling window, every entry gated on regime carries a leakage artifact. Verify regime labeling is strictly lagged.

High volume threshold fragility: volume_surge_threshold=1.9 was evolved on a specific asset/period. On new assets or bear regimes, vol ratio distributions shift, and this threshold may be chronically unsatisfied, generating zero trades in OOS.

Linear regression slope collinearity: slope is computed on the same bars that precede the squeeze release. In choppy releases the slope may point up even as price immediately reverses; adding close > VWAP or dist_from_high_20 > threshold as confirmation would mitigate.

ATR-denominated stop on a high-volatility asset: atr_14 during the squeeze compression period is itself suppressed (that is the squeeze condition). The stop is set at close - 3.79 * suppressed_ATR, which may be tighter than intended when ATR reverts to normal post-release, causing premature stop-outs.

bb_bw_percentile lookahead: the percentile is computed over a 100-bar rolling window. The first 100 bars have incomplete percentile estimates (min_periods=20). If the backtester warm-up of 50 bars is shorter than this, early percentile values are computed on fewer than 100 bars and may be systematically biased low, creating phantom squeezes in the earliest data.

Overfitting to 2021 bull run: the strategy evolved from 2019-2026 data but crypto had extreme squeeze-then-rally setups in 2020-2021. If most of the 49 profitable trades cluster there, the OOS 2024-2026 period may not reproduce the pattern.

Time stop destroys long-term breakouts: the 36h time stop will forcibly exit multi-day moves. In the long-only context, the best squeeze releases run for 5-10 days. Extending time_stop_hours to 96-120 is critical but increases sensitivity to parameter choice.

Cross-asset generalization: if the genome was evolved primarily on BTC/ETH data, the keltner_atr_mult=1.1686 and volume_surge_threshold=1.9 may be asset-specific. The cross-asset generalization penalty in the fitness function helps but does not eliminate this risk.
